slides.title: What we built in one sentence
slides.subtitle: Name the user, action, and outcome.

visual_aid.purpose: Tell judges what they are about to see.

speaker_notes.key_message: Judges should know the project immediately.

slides.title: The demo shows the highest-value path

visual_aid.purpose: Anchor the pitch in a repeatable live path.

validation.expected: Presenter can run or show the demo path without changing slides.
validation.fallback: Use a screenshot or recorded clip if live demo fails.

speaker_notes.key_message: The demo is the proof, not a feature tour.

slides.title: The build has a real technical core
body: Show the smallest architecture slice that proves implementation.
slides.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  A[Input] --> B[Core module]
  B --> C[Validated output]

visual_aid.purpose: Prove technical execution without overloading the judges.

speaker_notes.key_message: Technical depth should be simple enough for judges to believe in seconds.

slides.title: The project maps directly to the judging criteria

speaker_notes.key_message: Judges should see the project through their rubric.

slides.title: Close
slides.quote: The project is small enough to demo and real enough to extend.
slides.no_claims_reason: Closing synthesis; replace with sourced claim if factual.

visual_aid.purpose: Leave judges with one repeatable sentence.

speaker_notes.key_message: Close with the project's remembered sentence and next step.
